Aslrology and Vocational Guidance
Judgment of suitable vocation from astrological considerations alone
is a risky and difficult undertaking. IN modem times. the number of
different kinds of employment available is so great that it is almost
impossible to accommodate arr of them within the framework of twelve
planets and twelve houses. The field is vast and much experience is
necessary before the astrologer can venture to give spedfic advice
regarding a particular vocation. Hence it is not possible to deal with the
subject adequately within the limits of a magazine article, and the
following may only be regarded as a summal'y.
It is the practice of India astrologers to p~ attention to the 6th
and 10111 llouses onty and neglect other parts of the horoscope. This is
only a superlidal way at looking at the problem. The horoscope should
be studied as a whole and all the relevant factors shouk:J be taken into
account.
First examine where the majority of planets.
